channel.title: FuelWired — Oil & Gas Intelligence
channel.description: Real-time oil and gas industry news, energy market analysis, and LNG market intelligence.

item.title: Oil Jumps on Fears of Wider Iran War
item.description: Traders boosted bullish oil bets as geopolitical tensions increased risks to global energy and fuel markets.

item.title: Bangladesh's $12.65 Billion Nuclear Bet Faces Its Biggest Test Yet
item.description: The world is turning toward nuclear power as a solution to the increasingly dire energy trilemma – the tricky issue of sourcing energy that is simultaneously affordable, sustainable, and secure. As the artificial…

item.title: Colombia's New Government Prepares Fossil Fuel Comeback
item.description: On 21st June, conservative, Trump-endorsed presidential candidate Abelardo de la Espriella won the election by just 1 per cent over Iván Cepeda, who was expected to take over the Socialist Party leadership from current…

item.title: Chevron, Cheniere Reps Join DOE SEAB Board
item.description: The U.S. Department of Energy announced newly appointed members of the Secretary of Energy Advisory Board.

item.title: BP and Conoco Partner in One of Iraq's Oldest Oilfields
item.description: BP said ConocoPhillips will acquire 42 percent of its stake in a venture that's redeveloping Iraq's giant Kirkuk oil fields.

item.title: Zelenskyy Picks Energy CEO for Premier
item.description: President Volodymyr Zelenskyy named the head of a state-run energy company as Ukraine's next prime minister.

item.title: ADES Holding wins $229m offshore drilling contracts in Nigeria and UK
item.description: ADES Holding has secured two offshore drilling contracts totalling around $229.1m for projects located in Nigeria and the UK North Sea.

item.title: India Proposes Stricter Vehicle Emission Rules To Cut Oil Consumption
item.description: The Indian government has proposed stricter fuel efficiency regulations for passenger vehicles under the Corporate Average Fuel Efficiency (CAFE)-III norms, with the new fuel standards set to take effect on April 1…

item.title: New Jersey Bets Big on Small Nuclear Reactors
item.description: The newest frontier for the global nuclear renaissance is none other than New Jersey. This month, the state officially enacted a law launching a competitive procurement process to review and select a minimum of 1,100…

item.title: The Middle East to lead global methanol capacity additions by 2030
item.description: The Middle East is poised to dominate global methanol capacity additions by 2030, driven by its access to abundant and cheap natural gas.

item.title: BP, ConocoPhillips Partner In Iraq’s Giant Oilfield
item.description: ConocoPhillips (NYSE:COP) agreed to acquire a 42% stake in BP Plc’s (NYSE:BP) development subsidiary covering four major oilfields in the Kirkuk region of northern Iraq, with the partnership aiming to rehabilitate and…

item.title: US Oil Drilling Picks Up As Brent Gains 4%
item.description: The total number of active drilling rigs for oil and gas in the United States rose this week, according to new data that Baker Hughes published on Friday, bringing the total rig count in the US to 588, up 44 from this…

item.title: EIA Pushes 2026 USA Oil Output Forecast Closer to 14MM Bpd
item.description: The U.S. Energy Information Administration raised its U.S. crude oil production forecast for 2026 in its latest short term energy outlook.

item.title: Jeff Currie: Illusion of Oil Abundance Is Gone
item.description: Carlyle Group Chief Strategy Officer, Jeff Currie, has declared that the global oil market's "illusion of abundance" has vanished, warning that the market has rapidly transitioned from a standard supply deficit into a…

item.title: Oil Markets Ignore Mounting Risks at Their Own Peril
item.description: Escalating U.S.-Iran hostilities, attacks on critical infrastructure, and near-halted Hormuz tanker traffic are fueling expectations of higher oil prices. Friday, July 17, 2026 US forces striking civilian infrastructure…

item.title: Trump-Backed Trade Corridor Moves Closer to Reality in Armenia
item.description: The Armenian government approved a bill on July 16 to formalize the country’s role in the development of the Trump Route for International Peace and Prosperity, or TRIPP, which is envisioned as a key node in the…

item.title: Oil prices rise as US-Iran hostilities threaten major shipping routes
item.description: Oil prices rose slightly on Friday following increased military actions between the US and Iran in the Gulf region, with concerns mounting over potential disruptions to key oil transit routes, reported Reuters.

item.title: Halliburton wins multi-year LSTK onshore contracts from Aramco
item.description: Halliburton has secured multi-year lump sum turnkey (LSTK) contracts from Aramco for work across several onshore fields in Saudi Arabia.

item.title: U.S. Expands Iran Strikes as Tehran Retaliates Across the Gulf
item.description: US forces stepped up attacks on Iranian sites, reportedly striking key bridges, railways, and an airport, prompting retaliatory action by Tehran, even while the White House said talks between the warring sides were…

item.title: Geopolitical Risk Premium Returns as Crude Posts Biggest Weekly Gain in Months
item.description: September WTI crude oil posted its strongest weekly gain in months, rallying over 11% as traders rapidly rebuilt a geopolitical risk premium into the market. After opening the week near $72.50, prices climbed above $80…
